title: approvals — trait composition
description: Two owned traits. `HasApprovalHistory` is mixed into the `User` model — exposes convenience relations for the caller's approvals-requested, approvals-pending-review, approvals-decided history without pulling in the whole ApprovalPresenter. `IsApprovable` is a MARKER trait on subject types that CAN be the subject of an approval instance (any resource type an action targets — e.g. Invoice, Grant, Athlete). Optional: most callers don't need it — the `approve` middleware works off the action's `#[AsApprovableAction]` attribute, not the subject. The trait is only used when a subject wants a first-class `->approvals()` inverse relation for admin UIs.

owned.description: Mixed into the `User` model (via user module composition) to expose three convenience relations without materialising the ApprovalPresenter. Read-only accessors — never writes. Every method scopes by tenant + application via the composing model's `BelongsToTenant` global scope so cross-tenant leakage is impossible.
usedBy: user::User (exposes approval-history relations on the caller model)

provides_methods.description: The caller's own submitted requests — every `approval_instances` row where `requester_id = user.id`. Ordered `requested_at DESC` by default. Filtered via composing model's BelongsToTenant + application scope.

Instances where the caller sits in `approval_requirements.resolved_approvers` on at least one pending requirement (status='pending'). Backed by a JSONB containment query (`resolved_approvers @> to_jsonb(user.id)`) so it uses the GIN index on the column. Returns a Builder (not a HasMany) because the join relationship is polymorphic on the JSONB payload.
Every `approval_decisions` row where `approver_id = user.id`. Includes both approves + rejects. Used by 'my activity' feed. Ordered `decided_at DESC`.

owned.description: Marker trait on subject types (Invoice, AccessGrant, Athlete, RoleAssignment) that can be referenced by an `approval_instances.subject_type + subject_id` pair. Adds a polymorphic inverse `->approvals()` relation. OPTIONAL — the approval engine doesn't require the subject to compose this trait (subject is just a polymorphic tuple in the DB). Compose only when a subject-type-specific admin UI needs to render 'all approvals touching this resource' without a bespoke query.

provides_methods.description: Reverse polymorphic relation matching `approval_instances.subject_type = static::class + approval_instances.subject_id = this->id`. Ordered `requested_at DESC`.

Convenience accessor for `approvals()->where('status', 'pending')->first()` — used by policy checks that need to short-circuit 'this resource is currently locked pending an approval flow' without duplicating the query.

framework.reason: ApprovableAction, ApprovalTemplate, ApprovalInstance. NOT ApprovalRequirement, ApprovalDecision, ApprovalReminder (append-only + high-volume; hard-purged on retention).

approvable_action.notes: NO BelongsToTenant — approvable_actions is a PLATFORM-level registry (populated at boot by discovery scanning `#[AsApprovableAction]` decorators). Every tenant sees the same registry; per-tenant enable/disable is done via approval_templates.is_active, not by cutting rows here.

approval_template.notes: Tenant-scoped. Auditable because template edits are governance-critical (someone changed the refund-approval rules → auditor needs to see the diff). SoftDeletes because template deletion is a rare + reversible admin action; hard delete only via cascade on tenant erasure. Ordering of traits: BelongsToTenant FIRST so subsequent trait booted() hooks observe tenant context.

approval_template_approver.notes: Tenant-scoped via parent template's tenant_id (denormalised on the row for FK integrity + query-plan). NO SoftDeletes — template group edits bump the parent template's version so history lives at the template level, not per-group.

approval_instance.notes: Tenant-scoped. Auditable + HasActivityLog BOTH — compliance signal (field-level diffs via Auditable) + product feed (human-readable events via HasActivityLog). SoftDeletes retains rejected/expired/cancelled instances for the 90-day compliance hold; hard delete via `approvals:purge-expired`.

approval_requirement.notes: Tenant-scoped. NO SoftDeletes / HasUserstamps / HasActivityLog / Auditable — requirements are internal engine state (materialised at instance-create time; frozen after decision). Cascade deletes with the parent instance. High-volume table (N requirements per instance × N instances per tenant) — traits kept minimal to hold row writes at ~1KB.

approval_decision.notes: Tenant-scoped. Decisions are effectively write-once (no updates, no soft-delete — a bad decision is retracted via a NEW approve/reject flow, not by editing the row). NO Auditable because the row IS the audit event (the decision is the record). Retained forever per compliance.

approval_reminder.notes: Tenant-scoped. Append-only reminder-dispatch audit. Zero domain semantics beyond 'we sent reminder N to approver X at time Y'. NO SoftDeletes / HasUserstamps / HasActivityLog. Purged with the parent instance on retention hold.
